titlestmt.titleproper: Delta Tau Delta, Beta Omicron chapter records37-4-1002
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Herbert Finch

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-24 23:15:23 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Delta Tau Delta, Beta Omicron chapter records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Delta Tau Delta Fraternity. Beta Omicron Chapter (Cornell University)

did.physdesc: Correspondence, plans, pamphlets, printed material, the original charter of the fraternity, photographs.
did.abstract: Includes correspondence, plans, pamphlets and other printed material; also, the original charter of the fraternity. Major correspondents are Malcolm J. Freeborn '29, and Edward J. Trethaway '49. Includes photographs of the various houses.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: Beta Omicron Chapter of Delta Tau Delta was incorporated on January 25, 1890 at Cornell University. The original house was the old Ezra Cornell home (Forest Park).

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Since the Delta Tau Delta records came from several sources, the collection was arranged into two chronological series: the undergraduate chapter, Beta Omicron, and the alumni chapter which organized as the House Corporation and functioned with trustees. Both chapters dealt extensively with the national (arch) chapter. The primary collectors were Malcolm J. Freeborn '29 and Edward J. Trethaway '49.

The bulk of the records were created during the 1960's when there was much discussion of the role of fraternities on the campus, changing social patterns, race relations and civil rights. The collection documents the fraternity's role in pressing Cornell to develop a small group housing policy including resident advisors and the efforts of the alumni chapter to raise money for and to build a new shelter. The correspondence with the membership during the final months of the campaign (primarily the spring and summer of 1964) is arranged alphabetically to show the response of individuals to the effort. Samples were kept of the actual pledge cards and of their gathering and transmission to the University. There is a file of "The Cordelt," and there are copies of Donald L. Mallory '17, "80 Years at Cornell, 1890-1970. A History of Delta Tau Delta Fraternity at Cornell University."

Besides Freeborn and Trethaway, major correspondents are: Joseph S. Barr '18, John C. Dorfman '48, William R. Ebersol '45, Louis C. (Whitey) Edgar '33, Robert A. Eyerman '32, George B. Marchev '43, Reed L. McJunkin '32, Warner D. Orvis '06, V. Stewart Underwood '43, officers of the national chapter including: Alfred P. Sheriff and Frederick D. Kershner and officers of Cornell University including: Walter Bruska, John E. Burton, Deane W. Malott, Albert S. Miles, Neal Stamp, John Summerskill, Charles E. Warwick and J. L. Zwingle.

p.extref: Edward J. Trethaway Papers, #41-5-2617. Divison of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Delta Tau Delta Records, #37-4-1002. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to building plans restricted to permission of the office of Planning, Design and Construction.

did.unittitle: Activities: Rushing, reunion, summer jobs, etc.
Activities: Peter Schneck's shelter design project, 1959
Miscellaneous publications including: Donald L. Mallory '17, "80 Years at Cornell, 1890-1970. A History of Delta Tau Delta Fraternity at Cornell University."
Relations with the Arch Chapter (National Office), Secret matter
Relations with the Eastern Division, Form letters, 1966-1969
Photographs, Formal parties and Spring Day group shots, 1947, 1948, 1949
Certificate of Incorporation [photostat], June 7, 1890, approved and filed June 16, 1890. Constitution and By-Laws, 1905, 1911, 1920, n.d., n.d., 1963, 1964. Correspondence relative to amendments.
Agenda and minutes of meetings, 1954-1959. Occassionally minutes of other groups and sometimes correspondence and documents are attached. See 1957 for a housing proposal, for example.
Agenda and minutes of meetings, 1960-1964. See 10/22/60 for Warner Orvis' financial history of the local chapter.

"Our Future Image at Beta Omicron Chapter of Delta Tau Delta Fraternity," by Malcolm J. Freeborn, June 19, 1965. The publication of a speech delivered upon his resignation as president of the House Corporation. Correspondence included.
Correspondence, 1968, visit of Justice Tom C. Clark
Stewart Howe Alumni Service, Position papers
Cornell Intrafraternity Alumni Association/Resident Fraternity Advisors, 1958-1970
Clippings and Statistics relating to fraternities, 1962-1965
Information, 1961; "Solicitor Instructions," "Donor Information," "Finance Committee Report, New Chapter House"
Dedication, "Forest Park," by Professor Stephen W. Jacobs
Whiteprints, May 2, 1963, "F" July 9, 1963, "G" August 1, 1963, "I" October 23, 1963, "No.12" February 18, 1965 [24 p.]
Old shelter, 110 Edgemoor Lane, Appraisal, Warren Real Estate, May 6, 1963

"Appraisal of the Delta Tau Delta House..., May 18, 1912;" The American Appraisal Company, Milwaukee, Wisconsin. Includes a full description of the building and all furnishings. Bound volume, 91 pages plus measured drawings.
Plaque, "In Memory of Willard D. Straight '01, Donated by Joseph D. Trethaway '19 and Edward J. Trethaway '49"
Forest Park, 10 photographs, 11"x13" (Home of Ezra Cornell and his descendants, 1957 to 1929 and site of new shelter).
Old house/new house memento with correspondence, 1966
Photograph, 1947, of Otto, Prager[?], Orvis, and Wigton
Printed copy of photograph of Delta Tau Delta, 1896-1965, at 110 Edgemoor Lane, bought by Cornell 1965 and torn down
Photographs of Delta Tau Delta house, 110 Edgemoor Lane, interior and exterior
Negatives of Delta Tau Delta house, 110 Edgemoor Lane, interior and exterior
